description: Wire each migrated frontend to its backend: add Next /api proxy routes (or a typed @quant/api-client) pointing at the app's canonical *_BACKEND_URL, forward Authorization, and mount data providers. Today these apps fetch relative /api/* or hardcoded hosts with no server behind them.
steps: For each app, create src/app/api/** route handlers proxying to the Fastify backend (pattern from Phase 66 / apps/quantmail/src/app/api), using the canonical *_BACKEND_URL default from Phase 83 FEAT-003 and forwarding Authorization headers.

Replace ad-hoc fetch('/api/...') in hooks with @quant/api-client (typed React Query SDK) where practical, or with NEXT_PUBLIC_*_API_URL-driven fetches; remove hardcoded hosts (e.g. relative '/api' with no server, or stray app-specific domains).
Ensure each backend route the hooks call actually exists; if a hook calls an endpoint with no backend route, add the route in apps/<app>/backend/routes (or fix the path) so the call resolves.
Verify auth token flow end-to-end (token from login -> Authorization header -> Phase 83 FEAT-002 enforcement).
acceptance_criteria: Each app has working /api proxy routes pointing at its real backend port
No frontend fetch targets a nonexistent host/path; every hook endpoint has a backend route
Authorization headers forwarded; auth works end-to-end
